issue_summary: Implement method to add all stream elements into a PriorityQueue.
javadoc_before:
/** * Adds all elements of the collection into the queue. This method should be preferred over * calling {@link #add(Object)} in loop if all elements are known in advance as it builds queue * faster. * *

If one tries to add more objects than the maxSize passed in the constructor, an {@link * ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException} is thrown. */
javadoc_after:
/** * Adds all elements of the collection into the queue. This method should be preferred over * calling {@link #add(Object)} in loop if all elements are known in advance as it builds queue * faster. * *

If one tries to add more objects than the maxSize passed in the constructor, an {@link * ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException} is thrown. Which may result in parts of elements added into the * queue, but the heap is still stay in correct state. In this case, if caller wants to readd or * {@link #updateTop(Object)} with remaining elements, it should skip(continue) consumed elements * with the delta size of queue. */
